### Lesson Coverage (Lessons 1–5)

- **Lesson 1: a /ă/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 2: m /m/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 3: s /s/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 4: t /t/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 5: VC & CVC** — Core: at · Fringe: mat, sat

---

### Full TPT Description

**Give every complex communicator full access to UFLI Foundations — starting at Lesson 1. Free.**

If your student uses AAC and your school uses UFLI Foundations, you already know the problem: every step of the lesson assumes the student can speak. This companion removes that barrier without removing a single cognitive demand.

Same phonemes. Same sequence. Same rigor. Different output mode.

---

**What's Inside (5 Lesson Packets + Data Tracker)**

Each packet includes:
- Symbol cards — ARASAAC pictograms for every word, labeled core or fringe, color-coded by Fitzgerald Key category to match your student's AAC system
- Lesson at-a-glance — phoneme, grapheme, new words, review words, and heart words on one page
- Review words — organized by Fitzgerald Key color category so your communication partner can pull from the student's growing binder
- Heart words — irregular words for the lesson, with the sound/spelling mismatch flagged
- Session data tracker — prompt level by UFLI step, core word use, connected text, mastery decision

Also included: AAC Phonics Data Tracker (Excel) — a running compilation spreadsheet that collects data across all lessons in one place.

**Lessons covered:**
- **Lesson 1: a /ă/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 2: m /m/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 3: s /s/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 4: t /t/** — letter introduction (no new phoneme-grapheme words)
- **Lesson 5: VC & CVC** — Core: at · Fringe: mat, sat

---

**Access Methods Supported**

Direct selection · Eye gaze · Partner-assisted scanning · Switch scanning · Head pointer · Adapted keyboard · SGD (any system) · Low-tech AAC (PECS, e-trans board, symbol cards) · No-tech

---

**What You'll Need Next**

These packets give you the lesson-specific materials. The step-by-step scripts, prompting framework, auditory confirmation loop instructions, and pacing guidance are in the **UFLI AAC Companion: Lessons 6–34 + Teacher & Partner Guide** (available in this store for $20). That product includes everything — including these Lessons 1–5 packets — so full-set buyers only download once.

**Grade Range:** PreK–12+ (access-based, not grade-based)
**Subjects:** Special Education · Speech Therapy · Reading · Structured Literacy
**Price:** FREE

### Lesson-by-Lesson Coverage

- **Lesson 6: p /p/** — Core: at · Fringe: ap, map, mat, pat, sap, sat, tap
- **Lesson 7: f /f/** — Fringe: fat, map, sap, sat, tap
- **Lesson 8: i /ĭ/** — Core: if, it · Fringe: fit, pim, pit, sip, sit, Tim, tip
- **Lesson 9: n /n/** — Core: in, it · Fringe: fin, fit, map, nap, nip, pin, pit, sap, sip, sit, tap, tin, tip
- **Lesson 10: CVC Practice** — Core: at, if, in, it · Fringe: fat, fin, fit, map, mat, pat, pin, pit, sap, sat, sit, tap, tin · Morphology: letter→sound
- **Lesson 11: Nasalized /ă/** — Core: am, an, at · Fringe: Fan, man, Pam, pan, pat, Sam, sat, Tam, tan · ★ Heart: an · Morphology: an→Am
- **Lesson 12: o /ŏ/** — Core: not, on · Fringe: fat, map, mat, mop, nop, op, pat, pop, pot, sat, tap, top, tot
- **Lesson 13: d /d/** — Core: not · Fringe: ad, dot, fad, mad, mod, nod, pad, pod, pot, sad, sod, tad
- **Lesson 14: c /k/** — Core: can · Fringe: cam, cap, cat, cod, con, cop, cot, map, mat, tap
- **Lesson 15: u /ŭ/** — Core: not, up · Fringe: cup, cut, dun, fun, nut, pin, pit, pot, pun, sum, sun, un, us
- **Lesson 16: g /g/** — Core: got, not · Fringe: cot, cut, dig, dog, dot, dug, fig, gap, gas, gum, gut, mug, nag, nap, nod, nop, pig, pug, sag, tag, tug

---

### Full TPT Description

**The complete AAC access layer for UFLI Foundations Lessons 6–34. Everything your team needs to run structured literacy with a complex communicator — from the daily lesson table to the IEP meeting.**

Same phonemes. Same sequence. Same rigor. Every student. No speech required.

---

**What's Inside**

**29 Per-Lesson Packets (Lessons 6–34)**

Each packet includes:
- Symbol cards — ARASAAC pictograms for every word, labeled core or fringe, color-coded by Fitzgerald Key category to match your student's AAC system
- Lesson at-a-glance — phoneme, grapheme, new words, review words, and heart words at a glance
- Review words — organized by Fitzgerald Key color categories, ready to pull from the student's growing binder
- Heart words — irregular words for the lesson, with the sound/spelling mismatch flagged
- Morphology notes — where UFLI words are tense variations (sit/sat, has/had), flagged as a teaching moment
- Session data tracker — prompt level by UFLI step, core word use, connected text, mastery decision

**Teacher Guide + Communication Partner Guide**
- All 8 UFLI steps adapted for complex communicators — alternative response pathways and access options for every AAC modality
- Step-by-step scripts for every UFLI step — what to say, what to present, how long to wait
- Auditory confirmation loop — your student hears every phoneme confirmed back regardless of how they respond
- Aided Language Input Map — when and how to model on the device during phonics
- Prompting framework — System of Least Prompts + Constant Time Delay, from Pre-Alphabet through independent reading
- Pacing guidance — mastery over pace, when to move on, when to stay
- IEP goal stems — language growth goals, not compliance goals
- Research base — 8 peer-reviewed citations

**Also Included**
- AAC Phonics Data Tracker (Excel) — running compilation spreadsheet across all lessons
- Lessons 1–5 packet (free product included) — so full-set buyers have the complete sequence in one download

---

**Access Methods Supported**

Direct selection · Eye gaze · Partner-assisted scanning · Switch scanning · Head pointer · Adapted keyboard · SGD (any system) · Low-tech AAC (PECS, e-trans board, symbol cards) · No-tech

---

**Why This Product Exists**

No other product on TPT provides a complete access layer for all 8 UFLI Foundations lesson steps for AAC users. The phonemic processing demand is intact. Your student still hears, holds, and matches the phoneme. The only thing that changes is the motor output required to respond. Removing the oral production barrier is not reducing rigor.
